methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Andrenam is a seed-stage defense and security company founded by Matej Cernosek and Alex Chu that has raised $10M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 private and 2 public peers in the defense and security sector, including seed and Series A stage companies.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.5x multiplier to the $10M raised as a proxy for current value, implying a $25M valuation consistent with typical seed-to-next-stage step-ups. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector comparables—Tenna Systems ($60M seed), Ares Industries ($150M Series A), Vatn Systems ($300M Series A), and Stark Defence ($500M Series A)—yields a median valuation of $225M, with a range spanning $60M to $500M. These peers collectively inform market pricing for early-stage defense tech.

The two methods produce a wide triangulated range: $21M at the low end, $125M at the median, and $259M at the high end. The disparity reflects the substantial uncertainty inherent in early-stage venture valuations and the heterogeneity of Series A pricing across the sector. The low-end estimate aligns with stage-progression mechanics; the high-end is anchored to top-tier Series A comparables. Key uncertainties include Andrenam's specific technology differentiation relative to peers, customer traction, and the timing and competitive intensity of its next funding round. A $50M–$150M range may better reflect realistic downside and base-case scenarios given seed-stage risk.
